title: senna artemisioides subsp. artemisioides
intro: Senna artemisioides subsp. artemisioides, also known as bush senna or Australian cassia, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a laxative due to the presence of anthraquinone glycosides. The health benefits of Senna artemisioides subsp. artemisioides include its potential to treat constipation and other digestive issues, as well as its use in traditional medicine to reduce fever and inflammation. Senna artemisioides subsp. artemisioides can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support digestive health and promote regular bowel movements.
